Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
scheme: ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Sharmila D'Silva is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹11,18,142 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 25 Jun 2026 is ₹119.69.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

text:
[ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund – Direct Growth - Exit Load]
Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Rajat Chandak is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹11,18,142 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 25 Jun 2026 is ₹20.88.
The ICICI Prudential Flexicap Fund Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹5,000. Exit load of 1%, if redeemed within 1 month.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth - Exit Load]
Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

About ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth
ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Sharmila D'Silva is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth fund. The fund currently has an A
ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Sharmila D'Silva is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹11,18,142 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 25 Jun 2026 is ₹382.14.
The ICICI Prudential Midcap Direct Plan Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100.  Exit load of 1% if redeemed within 1 year.

scheme: ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Midcap Fund – Direct Growth - Exit Load]
This scheme aims to generate long term capital appreciation by investing in diversified midcap stocks portfolio. It will predominantly invest in companies with market capitalization falling between the lowest and the highest market capitalisation among the constituents of Nifty Midcap 100 Index.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: ICICI Prudential Indo Asia Equity Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Indo Asia Equity Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
scheme: ICICI Prudential Indo Asia Equity Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential Indo Asia Equity Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Exit load

Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

The Expense Ratio of ICICI Prudential Smallcap Fund Direct Plan Growth is 1.14% as of 30 Jun 2026...

The AUM, short for
Assets Under Management
of ICICI Prudential Smallcap Fund Direct Plan Growth is ₹8,948.97Cr as of 30 Jun 2026.
How to Redeem ICICI Prudential Smallcap Fund Direct Plan Growth?
If you want to sell your ICICI Prudential Smallcap Fund Direct Plan Growth holdings, go to your holding on the app or
web and simply click on it. You will get two options - redeem & invest more; click on redeem and enter your
desired amount or if you wish to redeem the entire holding amount then select the 'redeem all' checkbox.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth

[ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

text:
[ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth - Expense Ratio]
Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.
scheme: ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth

text:
[ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver – Direct Plan Growth - Exit Load]
Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

ICICI Prudential ELSS Tax Saver Direct Plan Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: HDFC Mid-Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Mid-Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: HDFC Mid-Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Mid-Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: HDFC Small Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Small Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: HDFC Small Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Small Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: HDFC Silver ETF FoF – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Silver ETF FoF – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
Sortino
scheme: HDFC Silver ETF FoF – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Silver ETF FoF – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
scheme: HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Fund – Direct Plan Growth

text:
[HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Fund – Direct Plan Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
scheme: HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Fund – Direct Plan Growth

text:
[HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Fund – Direct Plan Growth - Fund Overview]
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

[HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Fund – Direct Plan Growth - Expense Ratio]
Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

Advanced ratios
P/E (Price/ Earning) ratio
Weighted average of the P/E of underlying securities.     P/E is the ratio of the current share price to the company's earnings per share. Generally, overpriced stocks have a high P/E and underpriced stocks have a low P/E.
P/B (Price/ Book) ratio
scheme: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Expense Ratio]
Weighted average of the P/B of underlying securities.     It is the ratio of the current share price to the book value per share.     A lower P/B could either mean the stock is undervalued or there are problems in the company fundamentals.
Alpha
Weighted average of the alpha of underlying securities.     This indicates how a stock has performed compared to the benchmark index value.     If alpha is 1, it means the stock has outperformed the benchmark by 1% and -1 means the stock has underperformed by 1%.
Beta
Weighted average of the beta of underlying securities.     Beta measures the movement of a stock in relation to the overall market movement. If beta > 1, it means the stock is more volatile. If beta < 1, it means lower volatility.
scheme: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund – Direct Growth

text:
[HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund – Direct Growth - Fund Overview]
Sharpe ratio
Weighted average of the Sharpe ratio of underlying securities.     This helps understand the return of an investment compared to its risk. Sharpe ratio of 1 to 3 or above means greater returns from an investment but with a higher risk level.
Sortino
Weighted average of the sortino ratio of underlying securities to help evaluate the returns of an investment for a given level of bad risk.     Higher sortino ratio, better is the investment. Sortino ratio > 2 is considered to be good.

Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

Stamp duty
